# Button Builder development notes

- This repository is a Home Assistant custom integration plus a React/Vite visual editor for `custom:button-card` YAML.
- The canonical frontend lives under `src/`. `src/ButtonCardApp.tsx` owns application state; `src/components/` contains the editor and preview; `src/utils/yamlGenerator.ts` and `src/utils/yamlImporter.ts` handle YAML; `src/presets.ts` contains style presets.
- `src/types.ts` defines `ButtonConfig`, state appearance types, and defaults. New options must be reflected in the editor, preview where applicable, YAML generation/import, presets, AI schemas, and documentation.
- Binary entities use independent ON/OFF appearance buffers. Theme keys remain global. Preserve the merge/migration contract in `src/utils/stateAppearance.ts` and keep preview/YAML state output aligned.
- Effect intensity is defined in `src/utils/effectIntensity.ts`. Preview keyframes in `src/index.css` and generated keyframes in `src/utils/yamlGenerator.ts` must stay synchronized.
- Magic Builder supports Home Assistant AI Tasks and direct Gemini. Do not embed or log API credentials.
- The integration registers one iframe panel at `/button-builder` through `custom_components/button_builder/__init__.py`.
- Built assets under `custom_components/button_builder/www/` are generated by `npm run build`; do not hand-edit them.
- Keep versions in `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, and `custom_components/button_builder/manifest.json` identical for Home Assistant cache busting and HACS releases.
- Bubble Card, Tile Card, and other builders are planned as separate integrations and must not be reintroduced into this app shell.
- Before release, run `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm run build`, Python and JSON validation, and `git diff --check`.
